place: Fabius village, New York, New York

facts.text: Fabius takes its name from Roman general Quintus Fabius Maximus Verrucosus, a clever strategist famed for his war tactics.
The Fabius Historical Society sits in the village’s southeast corner, 20 miles south of Syracuse along former Skaneateles‑Hamilton Turnpike.
The village’s historic district was drawn to encompass the most coherent cluster of historic resources, preserving its architectural heritage.
Fabius Central School and Shea’s General Store appear in Jerome Mark Antil’s novel The Pompey Hollow Book Club, spotlighting local landmarks.
The Fabius Historical Society curates sports history, cataloging photographs and yearbooks from the Fabius‑Pompey school system dating back to 1946.
The Village of Fabius straddles NY‑80 and NY‑91, anchoring its center where these two state routes intersect.
